history.qwen_observation: The build failed during the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step due to a git ownership safety check triggered by the git-commit-id-plugin in the containerized environment.
history.qwen_rationale: Inserting a step to set git.commit.id.skip=true in the POM will prevent the git-commit-id-plugin from executing git commands during the build, bypassing the dubious ownership restriction and allowing the Spring Boot upgrade and subsequent steps to proceed.

history.qwen_observation: Build failed at `upgrade_spring_boot_3_3` (JDK 11) due to `git-commit-id-plugin` hitting Git's dubious ownership security check, compounded by Spring Boot 3.x requiring Java 17+ but being compiled under JDK 11.
history.qwen_rationale: The `git-commit-id-plugin` fails in containerized environments with strict Git ownership checks. Adding `git.skip` and `git.failOnUnableToExtractRepoInfo` properties bypasses the plugin entirely. Additionally, Spring Boot 3.x mandates Java 17; running the SB 3.3 upgrade step under JDK 17 ensures the compiler and runtime align with the new parent POM's `java.version=17` requirement, preventing post-recipe compilation failures.
